---
title: "Nonlinear DC & convergence aids"
subtitle: "T3 · Phase T3.3 · `va-core::{convergence,dc}`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: code complete — ladder rung 2.** The diode I–V sweep formally passes against real
QSPICE golden (2026-07-17, `t6-integration/03-validation.qmd`).
:::

## Goal

Solve circuits Newton alone struggles with — a stiff exponential device, a cold start far from
the answer — and sweep a source across a range of values.

## Where it fits

```
 newton::solve ──wrapped by──► gmin-stepping homotopy (convergence::gmin_for_step)
                                        │
                              dc::{operating_point, sweep}
```

## The idea

**Junction limiting keeps a diode/BJT from overflowing on a cold start** (`limit_junction`,
already introduced in `t3-core/02-newton.qmd`): a proposed forward-voltage step beyond `2·vt` of
the previous value is clamped logarithmically instead of taken raw — `exp(5.0/vt)` from a cold
`x=0` start would otherwise overflow before Newton ever gets a chance to converge.

**`gmin` stepping is a homotopy, not a single trick.** For a circuit plain Newton genuinely
cannot solve from `x=0` (a long chain of series diodes is the textbook stress case — chosen
deliberately, not as a token example), `NewtonConfig::gmin_steps > 0` wraps the inner iteration
in an outer ramp: solve a version of the circuit with a large conductance shunted from every
`Node`-kind unknown to ground (a strong, easy-to-solve linearizing aid), then geometrically
shrink that shunt down toward the real circuit's own answer, warm-starting each stage from the
previous one's solution. The schedule (`gmin_for_step`) decreases from `1e-3` S to a `1e-12` S
floor, then returns exactly `0.0` for the final stage — an ordinary, unshunted solve of the real
circuit.

**Only `Node`-kind rows are ever shunted** (`t3-core/01-mna.qmd`'s Node/Branch distinction) — a
voltage source's own constraint row is never touched, so `gmin` stepping is safe to enable
unconditionally, even on a circuit built entirely of ideal sources.

**A DC sweep is just "solve fresh from each new source value," deliberately not warm-started
between sweep points.** `dc::sweep` takes a `rebuild` closure that constructs a brand-new
instance set per swept value and solves each independently — the simplest correct
implementation, not the most efficient one. A production sweep could reuse the previous point's
solution as its own next warm start; this one doesn't, matching `va-core::dc::sweep`'s own
deliberate agnosticism about *what* changed between points (a source value, a parameter,
anything the caller's `rebuild` closure decides).

## The code

```rust
pub fn limit_junction(vnew: f64, vold: f64, vt: f64, vcrit: f64) -> f64;
pub fn default_vcrit(vt: f64) -> f64;    // derived the same way SPICE derives a diode's vcrit
pub fn gmin_for_step(step: usize, total_steps: usize) -> f64;

pub struct OperatingPoint { pub x: Vec<f64> }
pub fn operating_point(instances: &[&dyn ModelInstance], dim: usize, cfg: NewtonConfig)
    -> Result<OperatingPoint, CoreError>;
pub fn sweep<F>(points: &[f64], dim: usize, cfg: NewtonConfig, rebuild: F)
    -> Result<Vec<OperatingPoint>, CoreError>
    where F: FnMut(f64) -> Vec<Box<dyn ModelInstance>>;
```

`VT_NOMINAL`/`NOMINAL_IS`/`default_vcrit` are `va-core`'s own copy of the thermal-voltage
constant, deliberately *not* imported from `va_abi::reference::diode` — a generic Newton loop
shouldn't reach into one specific reference *device* for a physical constant that has nothing to
do with that device in particular.

## It works

```console
$ cargo test -p va-core convergence:: dc::
test convergence::tests::junction_limiting_bounds_step ... ok
test convergence::tests::default_vcrit_matches_silicon_diode_turn_on ... ok
test convergence::tests::gmin_schedule_is_monotone_and_terminates ... ok
test dc::tests::divider_operating_point ... ok
test dc::tests::sweep_divider_input ... ok

$ cargo test -p va-core gmin_stepping
test newton::tests::gmin_stepping_converges_a_circuit_plain_newton_cannot ... ok
test newton::tests::gmin_stepping_still_converges_the_diode_clamp ... ok
test newton::tests::gmin_stepping_does_not_corrupt_the_vsource_branch ... ok
```

`gmin_stepping_converges_a_circuit_plain_newton_cannot` is the real proof this isn't decorative:
a specific circuit is checked to *fail* under plain Newton and *succeed* with `gmin_steps > 0`,
in the same test — not just "gmin stepping runs without error."

The diode I–V sweep, driven through the real netlist pipeline (`t6-integration/{01-netlist,
02-cli}.qmd`) and compared against genuine QSPICE output, is ladder rung 2:

```console
$ cargo run -q -p xtask -- validate
[xtask]   PASS circuits/diode_iv.net: error=1.850e-16 (tol 1e-4)
```

(`t6-integration/03-validation.qmd` has an honest caveat on exactly what this comparison does
and doesn't exercise — the golden format records node voltage, not the diode's own current.)

## Limitations

**Junction limiting is blanket** (§ `t3-core/02-newton.qmd`) — the same tradeoff carries through
every convergence aid here, since none of them have per-device knowledge to sharpen with.

**`gmin` stepping's schedule is fixed**, not adaptive to how hard a particular circuit is to
solve — a circuit that needs more than the geometric ramp from `1e-3` to `1e-12` S provides would
still fail, just later than with plain Newton.

## What's next

`t4-transient/01-integration.qmd` — moving from a single operating point to a time-domain
transient.
